item.title: Accounting Officer
item.description: Accounting Officer (Temporary Agent, Grade AD5) IHI JU - Innovative Health Initiative Joint Undertaking Brussels, Belgium Please send us your application by no later than 14 May 2026 at 23:59 (Brussels time/Central European Time) , following the instructions in the ANNEX . The purpose of this call is to fill in one (1) vacant post and to establish a reserve list. 1. About IHI JU The Innovative Health Initiative (IHI) aims to translate health research and innovation into real benefits for patients and society, and to ensure that Europe remains at the cutting edge of interdisciplinary, sustainable, patient-centric health research. Health research and healthcare increasingly involve diverse sectors. By supporting projects that bring these sectors together, IHI will pave the way for a more integrated approach to healthcare, covering prevention, diagnosis, treatment, and disease management. IHI is a partnership between the European Union and European industry associations representing the pharmaceutical, medical technology, biotechnology, digital health and vaccine industries, namely COCIR, EFPIA, EuropaBio, MedTech Europe, and Vaccines Europe. IHI’s total budget is EUR 2.4 billion. Half of this comes from Horizon Europe, the EU’s research and innovation programme. The IHI industry partners have committed EUR 1 billion to IHI, and a further EUR 200 million can be committed by other organisations that decide to support the objectives of IHI in specific areas of research by becoming contributing partners. IHI builds on the successes of the Innovative Medicines Initiative, and the IHI Programme Office continues to manage projects launched under IMI. For more information, please visit the IHI JU website: www.ihi.europa.eu . 2. Job description The Accounting Officer will play a key role in ensuring the swift, accurate and timely delivery against specific deadlines of all tasks falling under the responsibility of the accounting function. S/he will act as the Accounting Officer for IHI JU, but also for other Joint Undertakings as part of the Accounting Back-Office Arrangement. Appointed by the relevant Governing Board/s, the Accounting Officer is independent in the performance of its duties, exercising due diligence and taking appropriate measures to safeguard the entities’ financial interests. The key responsibilities of the Accounting Officer will include, amongst others, the following: Accounting operations Ensuring the proper implementation of payments, collection of revenue and recovery of amounts established as being receivable in the accounts; Ensuring the accurate reconciliation and clearing of transactions; Ensuring the accurate registration and valuation of tangible and intangible assets. Accounting and financial reporting Preparing and presenting the annual accounts for the assigned Joint Undertakings in compliance with the applicable accounting and financial rules; Preparing VAT reporting for the Belgian authorities, where applicable; Preparing reports based on accounting and/or other financial data (cashflows; forecasts, etc.), where applicable. Compliance and internal control Monitoring compliance with the Financial Regulation as well as accounting rules and procedueres; Implementing the chart of accounts and accounting rules, as well as issuing instructions; Drawing up and keeping updated documents describing the organisation of the accounts and the accounting procedures of the joint undertaking; Laying down and validating the accounting systems and, where appropriate, validating systems laid down by the authorising officer to supply or justify accounting information; Liaising with key stakeholders, including the European Court of Auditors and external auditors concerning accounting matters; Ensuring compliance with the professional standards and requirements set by the European Court of Auditors; Advising on accounting and related financial matters. System and process optimisation Optimising the use of financial systems and accounting tools (SUMMA - SAP S/4HANA) including updating procedures and processes; Contributing to IT projects related to finance and accounting. 3. Eligibility criteria Candidates will be considered for the selection phase on the basis of the following criteria to be fulfilled by the closing date for applications: 3.1 General conditions be a national of a member state of the European Union; enjoy his/her full rights as a citizen 1 ; have fulfilled any obligations imposed by the applicable laws concerning military service; meet the character requirements for the duties involved; be physically fit to perform the duties linked to the post 2 ; as the predominant working language of IHI JU is English, candidates must have an excellent command of spoken and written English - level C1/C2 (note: native English speakers will be required to demonstrate the ability to work in a second EU language at interview stage). Candidates must also have a satisfactory knowledge (at least B2 level) of an

item.title: Head Climate and Circular Economy Policy (m/f/d)
item.description: Head Climate and Circular Economy Policy (m/f/d) thyssenkrupp Steel Europe AG Brussels, Belgium Employment Type: Full-time About the Role: thyssenkrupp Steel is seeking a highly motivated and experienced Head of Climate & Circular Economy Policy to join our EU Affairs team in Brussels. In this strategic, high-visibility role, you will lead the company’s engagement with EU institutions and key stakeholders on climate, circular economy, and sustainable steel policies - including green steel labeling and product policy initiatives. You will shape policy developments that enable the decarbonization of the steel sector while promoting Europe’s internationally competitive steel industry. Reporting to the CEO through the Government Affairs function, EU Affairs acts as the company’s frontline advocacy team in Brussels. Key Responsibilities: Develop and execute thyssenkrupp Steel’s EU government affairs strategy on climate, decarbonization, and circular economy; Lead advocacy and engagement on EU climate policy, circular economy initiatives, green steel labeling, environmental product requirements, and related regulatory matters; Represent thyssenkrupp Steel in discussions with policymakers, advocacy groups, industry partners, associations, and in external meetings, working groups, and conferences; Build and maintain strategic relationships with EU institutions, stakeholders, and industry networks to advance policy objectives; Collaborate with the Group Head of Climate Policy and internal teams to develop and align policy positions with business objectives and communicate updates to senior management; Draft and coordinate position papers, policy briefs, consultation responses, legislative amendment proposals, and presentations for internal and external audiences. Qualifications & Experience: 5–8 years of EU public affairs experience in relevant policy areas, with a well-established network of contacts in Brussels; Strong knowledge of climate policy, circular economy, and EU legislative processes; Proven ability to build and collaborate with strategic alliances, coalitions, trade groups, and stakeholder partnerships to advance policy goals; Strategic mindset with a proactive, results-oriented approach, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines; Excellent communication, interpersonal, and diplomatic skills with strong relationship management capabilities; Verbal and written fluency in English and German required; additional languages are an advantage; EU citizenship or valid work authorization for Belgium required. What We Offer: A central and highly visible role in shaping sustainable steel and industrial policies at the EU level; Opportunity to influence EU policy at the highest level, engaging directly with policymakers, regulators, and industry leaders; A dynamic, fast-paced environment where your strategic insights and advocacy skills will have direct, tangible impact; Competitive salary, benefits package, and opportunities for professional growth; Opportunity to engage with senior policymakers, industry leaders, and international stakeholders; Dynamic and collaborative work environment. How to Apply: Applications are accepted exclusively via the thyssenkrupp Steel job portal . If you have any questions about the advertised position or the application process, the HR team is available by email at hr.tksbs@thyssenkrupp-steel.com or by phone at +49 (0)203 523 23 23. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: f d thyssenkrupp Steel Europe

Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.

item.title: European Coordinator
item.description: European Coordinator BFFP - Break Free From Plastic Brussels, Belgium About you You are passionate about addressing plastic pollution and its root causes, and experienced with the coordination of groups, networks and/or projects. You enjoy solving problems and empowering collective work at the service of systemic change. You are good at anticipating challenges, jumping on opportunities, and quickly finding creative solutions, with a particular appetite for strategic thinking and building synergies, and are capable of running different tasks simultaneously. You excel at personal relations and enjoy team management; and you like working in an agile international environment with colleagues across the globe. About the role JOB SUMMARY The BFFP European Coordinator provides strategic and operational leadership to the BFFP Europe coordination team, overseeing the delivery of the European strategy across its key pillars. The role involves line managing senior staff, guiding collective planning and evaluation, and ensuring that the team works cohesively towards shared goals. The European Coordinator cultivates relationships with key external stakeholders, and raises the visibility of BFFP in European public life. A significant part of the role involves leading fundraising efforts, overseeing budgets and grant reporting, and maintaining strong funder relationships. The European Coordinator also plays a central role in connecting and aligning European work with BFFP's global strategy, and contributing to wider global work and team leadership. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES Coordination and management of the BFFP European coordination team Lead the European coordination team, including oversight of collective and individual planning and evaluation processes; Line manage three to four members of the team and support other line managers; Co-lead the recruitment of new staff and oversee their onboarding and offboarding as relevant Support the Senior Manager, People & Operations in setting and updating team policies and processes; Update on a regular basis the European Support Team (governance body) about team and movement development, and seek their input as relevant. Coordination of the effective implementation of the BFFP Europe strategy Oversee implementation of the different pillars (e.g. narrative, advocacy, movement building) of the European Strategy and support synergies building between pillars and projects, and between BFFP European movement and its policy arm the Rethink Plastic Alliance; Support the development of European-wide or joint campaigns or activities, notably through support to BFFP Task Force leaders and liaising with the Rethink Plastic Alliance Coordinator; Facilitate a collaborative evaluation of progress made collectively towards the achievements of the BFFP European strategy Support the resilience of the European movement; Co-lead the organisation of the BFFP European Meeting (typically every two years), along with the Senior Manager, People & Operations; Develop and maintain relationships with key external stakeholders as relevant (allies, change agents, decision-makers, funders); Pursue opportunities to raise the visibility of BFFP in public events, ensuring that BFFP members can represent the movement or representing it directly. Support cross-regional work and contribute to BFFP wider team processes Ensure that the European strategy aligns with and supports the achievement of the BFFP global strategy - Provide input for, contribute to planning, agenda setting and follow up, and participate in the Team Leads Forum (the movement's deliberative body comprised of team members who oversee main teams); Input to strategy & impact review processes on global level and to initiatives focused on resilience building; Coordinate fundraising efforts with those of the global team and of other regional teams; Identify and enhance synergies and complementarity between the work in Europe and in other regions or globally. Fundraising, budget and grant management Implement and lead BFFP Europe coordination fundraising strategy and oversee its implementation; Maintain strong relationships with funders; Oversee all BFFP Europe coordination related budgets; Oversee funding proposals, narrative and financial reporting; Support the Senior Manager, People & Operations, finance consultant, and key staff at the fiscal sponsor, in financial compliance and reporting. Person Specification Experience Essential: Experience in movements or networks; Substantial experience in coordination of groups, networks and/or projects; Experience in people management; Experience with budget and grant management; Strong understanding of issues associated with plastic along its supply chain; Experience in fundraising; Experience in working with people from different cultures and nationalities in a way that facilitates an intersectional approach. Desirable: Deep knowledge of the BFFP movement; Experience in advocacy, at national, EU and/or interna
item.dc:creator: BFFP Break Free From

item.title: Director Finance, Administration & IT
item.description: Director Finance, Administration & IT (Full-time) EURELECTRIC - Union of the Electricity Industry Brussels, Belgium We offer a unique opportunity to join the European trade association for electricity in a C-level position. The organisation: Speaking for more than 3500 companies, Eurelectric represents the full value chain of the European electricity industry in Brussels and beyond. Our priorities revolve around the full decarbonisation of the power sector, the deployment of electric solutions to decarbonise other parts of the economy and ensure competitiveness and energy security. The role: The Director – Finance, Administration & IT is responsible for the organisation’s financial governance and operational support functions, ensuring sound financial management, efficient administration, reliable IT systems and a coherent HR framework aligned with strategic priorities. Reporting to the Secretary General, the Director contributes to strategic planning and ensures that financial and operational systems effectively support the organisation’s activities. The role also provides financial analysis, operational oversight and risk management support to the Secretary General and governance bodies. Key Responsibilities 1) Finance & Administration Assisted by the Head of Finance, Administration & Payroll: Define and drive the organisation’s financial strategy, ensuring strong governance and longterm sustainability; Lead budgeting, forecasting and financial planning in alignment with strategic priorities; Ensure robust financial controls, compliance and integrity of accounting systems; Deliver financial insights, risk analysis and recommendations to senior leadership and governance bodies; Oversee financial reporting, audits and relationships with external partners (auditors, banks); Supervise administrative and operational support functions to ensure efficient internal operations; Drive process optimisation, resource efficiency and continuous improvement across support services. 2) Human Resources Assisted by the HR Manager: Provide strategic oversight of HR policies, people management and organisational development; Oversee recruitment, performance appraisal cycles, training and employee relations; Ensure compliance with labour legislation and internal HR frameworks. 3) Information Technology (IT) Assisted by 2 Senior IT advisors: Lead IT strategy and oversee infrastructure to ensure secure and efficient operations; Ensure maintenance, performance and evolution of systems, tools and digital platforms; Manage external IT providers and outsourced services; Oversee cybersecurity, data protection and business continuity; Drive digitalisation initiatives to enhance organisational efficiency. Profile and Competencies At least 10 years’ professional experience in financial governance, operational management at senior management level; Master in Finance, Economics, Business Administration or a related field; Strong knowledge of financial governance, accounting principles and internal control systems; Knowledge of IT infrastructure; Excellent leadership, communication and stakeholder management skills; Ability to contribute to strategic organisational development and support senior leadership; Fluency in English and French and/or Dutch. We offer A key role in a highly professional organisation with a positive, inclusive work culture; An attractive remuneration package, including a comprehensive range of benefits and an electric company car. Please submit your application, including a CV and motivation letter (quoting “ref. DFAI”) to grondinelli@eurelectric.org by 22/05/2026 . Attn. Giuseppina Rondinelli, HR Manager, Tel +32(0)471813802 Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: Union of the Electricity

item.title: Traineeship for Railways
item.description: Traineeship for Railways ETF - European Transport Workers' Federation Brussels, Belgium Do you want to play a crucial role in developing ETF’s involvement in Railways? Why join ETF? Do you want to contribute to the voice of 5 million + Transport Workers from 200+ Transport Unions in 38 countries? Do you want to promote the interests of transport workers in Europe as well as solidarity across borders? Are you pro Fair Transport? Are you committed to Trade Union values? Key Responsibilities Coordination: Support the coordination of the activities of the Railways Section; Support: Provide essential support to the Head of the Land Transport Department, the Senior Policy Officer for Railways, affiliates and the ETF Secretariat; Meeting Management: Organize, attend, support meetings and ensure effective follow-up in close cooperation with the Senior Policy Officer for Railways and dedicated Assistant; Research: Carry out in-depth pertinent research related to Railways; Policy: Draft policy documents, reports, position papers, minutes, etc.; SPOC: Be a point of contact for affiliates; Representation: Ensure ETF’s participation in events, representing ETF when required; EU Funding: Submit and implement EU co-funded projects; Collaboration: Cooperate with other ETF Departments and Communications Officers. Your Profile Education: Master’s Degree in EITHER Industrial Relations OR EU Law OR Political Sciences; Values: Experience and/or strong commitment to trade unions values; Language Skills: Fluent in English. Any other language is highly desirable; Communication: Strong communication skills with the ability to synthesize complex information; Proactivity: High level of initiative, independence and adaptability in order to work effectively in a small team; IT Skills: Proficient in MS Office; Travel: Willingness and availability to travel. Offer Compensation under a Belgian professional immersion contract of 1.056 €; Long traineeship of 6 to 12 months (exact duration to be discussed); Starting date: as soon as possible; Up to 2 days teleworking/week; Meal vouchers of 8 euros; Public transport subscription; 34 hours week, flexitime; A multicultural team of 25 persons; Dynamic workplace at the centre of Brussels; Weekly team meetings; European travels. Application Procedure We invite any interested candidate to send a CV and a short motivation letter in English to Agathe Smyth, ETF Senior Operations Manager, at hr@etf-europe.org . Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: ETF European Transport Workers

item.title: Policy Intern
item.description: Policy Intern ECF - European Cyclists' Federation Brussels, Belgium Internship description As an intern you will assist the advocacy team with doing research and policy analysis, and with lobbying the EU institutions to fulfil our mission. You will have an opportunity to apply and develop your abilities and interests in advocacy and research focused on sustainable mobility and cycling. Your line manager will be the Director for Advocacy and EU Affairs. Specific responsibilities for the Policy Intern may include Author a report analysing how Member States and competent regions transpose the relevant cycling parts of the Energy Performance of Buildings Directive EU 2024/1275 into national/regional law; Assist in the ECF campaign on the Multi-annual Financial Framework 2028-2034 and its possible impact on cycling investments; Set up and maintain a monitoring system to track the European Parliament’s committee work and other institutional activities relevant to cycling; Prepare meetings and take minutes; Assist with any type of ad-hoc measures in the advocacy team. Who are we looking for? Experience/Knowledge Essential Strong interest in transport policy; Knowledge in EU affairs; Bachelor or Master’s degree or be in your final year(s) to obtain your Master’s. Desirable Interest in the topic of bicycle parking; Previous working experience or volunteering in an advocacy role; Writing resources and materials for advocacy, political activity, cycling media, voluntary groups etc. Skills Essential Excellent English; Outstanding research skills; Strong writing skills; Good time management; Solid knowledge of MS Office; Ability to self-organise; Ability to pay attention to detail and take initiative in problem-solving. Desirable Other languages are an asset; Experience in policy analysis; Managing multiple tasks. Personal/Other Essential Interest and ability to work in a diverse cross-cultural environment; Share commitment to ECF’s values. Desirable Bicycle user for transport or leisure. Don't forget to mention EuroBrussels when applying.
item.dc:creator: ECF European Cyclists
